course_description:
Provides students with techniques for evaluating investments on an individual basis and in the context of portfolios. Techniques for analysing investments focus on maximising expected returns while minimising risk. The most powerful way of achieving this objective is by creating a portfolio of investments.

Topics covered are financial statement analysis, equity investments, debt investments, portfolio management, and macroeconomic and industry analysis.

After completing the course, students should be able to construct and evaluate investment portfolios on theoretical and practical grounds. FINM7403 Portfolio Management is integrated with many of the other finance courses (FINM7401, FINM7402, FINM7406, ACCT7106, and FINM7405), providing shared coverage of the fundamentals of investment markets, portfolio theory (the quantitative trade-off of risk and return), bond & equity valuation, risk-factor pricing, macroeconomic analysis, financial statement analysis and the basic features of derivative investments. The course expands on many of these topics in managing a portfolio of assets. It introduces other concepts relevant to portfolio management, such as portfolio performance evaluation and the efficient markets debate.

The course content addresses key questions of interest to potential investors:

The course is supplemented with a brief exploration of the Efficient Market Hypothesis, behavioural finance, and investments in financial derivatives. Finally, case studies will be used to complete the learning of the code of ethics and standards of professional conduct.

Alongside their study of financial theories and accompanying practical examples in this course, students will be exposed to relevant published academic studies and international case studies reflecting real-world practice. They will also be provided opportunities to source and process real data in R & Excel and AI tools (such as ChatGPT or Gemini) to enrich their understanding of portfolio management.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback In response to requests for more practical skills and authentic assessment, the course structure has been updated:

course_aims: This course aims to trainﾠstudents with techniques for evaluating investments on an individual basis and in the context of a portfolio. After completing the course, students should possess the theoretical and practical skills to value investments and construct investment portfolios.

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse and evaluate accounting, financial and non-financial information relevant to the task of asset allocation and security selection.
Assess the value of a financial asset using a variety of accepted methods.
Explain and evaluate the risks associated with investments on portfolio and security levels.
Evaluate the performance of a portfolio and portfolio manager.
Apply theory to the analysis of real world cases when working as part of a team, and employ databases and software commonly used in the industry.
Identify ethical dilemmas in various investment scenarios and explain the potential conflicts of interest that may arise in the investment industry.

assessment_details.title: Group Presentations and Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: Pitch deck submission 30/04/2026 4:00 pm Presentations I 1/05/2026 Presentations II 8/05/2026 Reflection 15/05/2026 4:00 pm
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Peer assessed, Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In this assessment, you will step into the role of a Fund Manager reporting to an Investment Committee. Drawing on the data generated during the Live Trading Simulation , you are required to deliver a professional presentation that defends your investment decisions and critically reflects on the outcomes. Format: Presentation (Live or Recorded): A professional pitch covering your investment philosophy, strategy execution, and performance attribution. Reflection on the Trading Game (Written ~1000 words) : This component requires you to critically evaluate your simulation experience by contrasting academic financial theory with the realities of practical portfolio management. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The Reflection component must be submitted for grading via the Blackboard Assignment submission link.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
The extension is only applied to the Reflection component. For in-class group presentations, no extension is permitted.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Topic 1: Mean-Variance Framework This topic develops understanding of risk and return, investor's preferences, and capital allocation to risky assets. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Topic 1: Mean-Variance Framework Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Topic 2: Capital Asset Pricing Model This topic develops the well-known Capital Asset Pricing Model and discuss its empirical challenges in explaining asset returns. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
Topic 2: Capital Asset Pricing Model Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05

Topic 3: Multifactor models: APT, ICAPM This topic discusses macroeconomic and industry analysis which is particularly useful in timing factor investments. It introduces a wide range of multi-factor asset pricing models to address the empirical challenges of the CAPM. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
Topic 3: Multifactor Models: APT, ICAPM Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
Topic 4: Performance Evaluation This topic discusses various methods to evaluate and understand investment fund performance/styles. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Topic 4: Performance Evaluation Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Topic 5: Fundamental Analysis This topic focuses on ratio analysis and equity valuation models including discounted cash flows and multiples. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
No tutorial due to public holiday No tutorial due to Good Friday public holiday
No lecture due to public holiday No lecture due to Good Friday public holiday
In-Semester Break
Topic 5: Fundamental Analysis Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
Trading Simulation I Live Trading Game - Part I - Initial Allocation Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Workshop Preparation Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Trading Simulation II Live Trading Game - Part II - Dynamic Allocation Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Group Presentations I Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Topic 6: Bond Portfolio Management This topic focuses on term structure of interest rates, management of bond portfolios, and bond risk factors. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Group Presentations II Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

Topic 7: Efficient Market Hypothesis and Behavioral Finance This topic discusses the notion of market efficiency and behavioural finance with applications on security returns. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Topic 6: Bond Portfolio Management Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L05
Topic 8: Options in Portfolio Risk Management This lecture discusses basics of options contracts and risk management strategies for portfolio investments. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Topic 7: Efficient Market Hypothesis and Behavioral Finance Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Topic 9: Guest Lecture on Special Topics Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Topic 8: Options in Portfolio Risk Management Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
Topic 10: Investment Process and Ethics & Course Review This lecture demonstrates the investment process and ethics in practice with CFA framework. Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05, L06
